channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Arizona prisoner serving 16 life sentences accused of killing 3 fellow inmates
item.description: An Arizona prisoner serving 16 life sentences allegedly killed three other inmates at a state prison, with officials saying he had "intent to harm.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Houston rallies to beat Duke 70-67 in the Final Four, will face Florida for the NCAA title
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Sorry guys, “Phi Slama Jama” is already taken. How ’bout something simple, like “Comeback Kids.” “National champs” might be a possibility, too.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Yemen Houthi rebels say US strikes kill 2; Trump's bombing video suggests higher overall death toll
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes killed at least two people overnight in a stronghold of Yemen's Houthi rebels, the group said Sunday, while a bombing video posted by U.S.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Jaguar and Land Rover maker pauses shipments to US as it develops post-tariff plans
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The maker of Jaguar and Land Rover cars is pausing shipments to the U.S. as Britain's struggling auto industry begins to respond to the 25% tax on vehicle imports imposed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Amanda Knox gives warning to students wanting to study abroad, 10 years after being acquitted of murder
item.description: Amanda Knox, a married mother of two, gives advice to students and their parents on whether study abroad is a safe option in 2025. She has a new book, "Free: My Search for Meaning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California culinary star dies after hiking in Hawaii
item.description: Gianna Buzzetta was killed in a freak accident in Kauai, Hawaii, while hiking with her boyfriend last week.
item.dc:creator: Jaime Chambers, NewsNation

item.title: Palestinian teenager who died in Israeli prison showed signs of starvation, medical report says
item.description: TEL AVIV (AP) — Starvation was likely the leading cause of death for a Palestinian teenager who died in an Israeli prison, according to an Israeli doctor who observed the autopsy.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas county that swung to Trump grapples with immigration crackdown after bakery is targeted
item.description: A recent immigration enforcement action that resulted in the arrests of employees and owners at a Texas bakery has shocked residents of the town of Los Fresnos.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Gonzalez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tariffs will make sneakers, jeans and almost everything Americans wear cost more, trade groups warn
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sending children back to school in new sneakers, jeans and T-shirts is likely to cost U.S. families significantly more this fall if the bespoke tariffs President Donald Trump put on leading exporters take effect as planned, American industry groups warn.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: No state has ended personal income taxes since 1980, but Mississippi and Kentucky may change that
item.description: About 45 years have passed since a U.S. state last eliminated its income tax on wages and salaries. But with recent actions in Mississippi and Kentucky, two states now are on a path to do so, if their economies keep growing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Storms tore up two of America's most iconic trails. Federal cuts have disrupted repairs
item.description: CAMPO, Calif. (AP) — Hiking the Pacific Crest Trail is a challenge, especially for adventurers making the entire run from Southern California to Canada, and Eric Kipperman’s job is to greet them at the start and lay bare the difficulties ahead.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Clayton leads Florida to NCAA title game, scoring 34 points in 79-73 victory over SEC rival Auburn
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — Walter Clayton Jr. is having an NCAA Tournament for the ages. Florida now just needs him to deliver one more time.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN HAWKINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Several University of Massachusetts international students have visas revoked
item.description: Five international students at the University of Massachusetts Amherst are the latest in the country to have their student visas revoked by the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Angry protesters from New York to Alaska assail Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies
item.description: Crowds of people angry about the way President Donald Trump is running the country marched and rallied in scores of American cities Saturday in the biggest day of demonstrations yet by an opposition movement trying to regain its momentum after the shock of the Republican’s first weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Judge awards $6.6 million to whistleblowers who reported Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton to FBI
item.description: A district court judge has ruled in favor of four whistleblowers who said that Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton retaliated against them for reporting bribery allegations, awarding his employees more than $6 million combined in damages.
item.dc:creator: Nadia Lathan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Cleveland's José Ramírez hits 3 homers and claims another piece of Guardians history
item.description: José Ramírez hit three homers and carried the Guardians to a victory Friday night. The slugger claimed another place in Cleveland baseball history in the process.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Takeaways from Trump's move to send Venezuelan migrants in the US to a prison in El Salvador
item.description: On Friday, March 14, President Donald Trump invoked the Alien Enemies Act, a 1798 law giving him immense powers to deport noncitizens in a time of war.
item.dc:creator: TIM SULLIVAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Increasing sleepiness linked to dementia risk in women, study finds
item.description: The research by Neurology discovered women in their eighties who experience sleep disruption have an association with cognitive aging.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump goes all in with bet that the heavy price of tariffs will pay off for Americans
item.description: President Donald Trump's new tariffs mark an all-in bet that his economic vision will pay off for Americans.
item.dc:creator: Zeke Miller And Steve Peoples, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate passes budget, setting up showdown with House over Trump agenda
item.description: Senate Republicans voted early Saturday morning to pass a budget resolution that will be critical to advancing President Trump’s legislative agenda, but the measure breaks with House Republicans on several big issues, setting the stage for a showdown between the two chambers later this year.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: At least 16 dead in flooding and tornadoes as storms slash from Texas to Ohio
item.description: Another round of torrential rain and flash flooding has come for parts of the South and Midwest already heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that also spawned some deadly tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: George Walker Iv And Bruce Schreiner, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Justice Department seeks 7 year sentence for George Santos
item.description: Prosecutors said the length of the requested sentence — 87 months — reflects the serious nature of Santos’s “unparalleled” crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KTLA 5

item.title: YouTube daredevil arrested by Indian authorities after visiting remote island where US missionary was killed
item.description: A YouTube influencer visiting a remote Indian island, where he allegedly tried to make illegal contact with indigenous people, has been arrested, according to global reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Actor Glen Powell launches organic condiments line
item.description: Actor and producer Glen Powell has launched Smash Kitchen, a line of organic condiments that includes non-GMO pantry essentials with remixed flavors, now exclusively available nationwide at Walmart stores and Walmart.com.
item.dc:creator: Erica Pauda, KRON4

item.title: Trump administration issues demands on Harvard as conditions for billions in federal money
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration has issued a list of demands Harvard University must meet as a condition for receiving almost $9 billion in grants and contracts, federal money that is being threatened during an investigation into campus antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: TSA officers find prohibited item in traveler's flip-flop at DC airport
item.description: TSA officers working at Washington Dulles on March 30 were able to stop a traveler from bringing the item through security, a spokesperson for the agency said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans are debating a higher tax rate for millionaires: Report
item.description: Republicans are reportedly considering raising taxes on millionaires to help offset the cost of their tax bill.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Divided Supreme Court sides with Trump to block teacher grants
item.description: A divided Supreme Court sided with the Trump administration by allowing officials to block $65 million in teacher development grants frozen over concerns they were promoting diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Baby boomers are buying more homes than millennials
item.description: Older buyers — who are more likely to purchase homes with cash — have been less affected by elevated mortgage rates.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Dow plunges 2,000 as sell-off for markets slams into a scarier gear following Trump's tariffs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The sell-off for financial markets worldwide is slamming into a higher, scarier gear on Friday. The S&P 500 tumbled 5.7% as Wall Street's worst crisis since the COVID crash deepened after China matched President Donald Trump’s big raise in tariffs announced earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: STAN CHOE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Texas measles outbreak approaches 500 cases
item.description: The deadly measles outbreak in Texas is continuing to rise, now approaching a total of 500 confirmed cases with at least 56 people facing hospitalization since late January, according to health officials.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Poor air quality increases depression risk
item.description: A new study finds poor air quality is linked to a heightened risk for depression. Depression is a classified as a mood disorder.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, NewsNation

item.title: US added 228,000 jobs in March as economy showed strength in buildup to Trump trade wars
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. employers added a surprising 228,000 jobs last month, showing that the American labor market was in solid shape as President Donald Trump embarked on a risky trade war with the rest of the world. The unemployment rate ticked up to 4.2%.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Five big questions the GOP's budget plan for Trump leaves unanswered
item.description: Senate Republicans are pushing to adopt a budget blueprint this week to kick off a complex process to advance President Trump’s tax agenda.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: A man has shot and killed a Catholic priest at his parish rectory in northeast Kansas
item.description: SENECA, Kansas (AP) — A Catholic priest was shot and killed Thursday by a man who approached him at his parish rectory in the town of Seneca, Kansas, church officials said. “I am heartbroken to share the tragic news of the death of Fr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dog saved after falling over 100 feet at Colorado National Monument
item.description: What started as a dog owner's worst nightmare ended in a heartwarming reunion thanks in part to a multi-agency effort by Colorado responders.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Kiser, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Pentagon watchdog to review Hegseth’s use of Signal app to convey plans for Houthi strike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon’s acting inspector general announced Thursday that he would review Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s use of the Signal messaging app to convey plans for a military strike against Houthi militants in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4
